what: hitop — R package for HiTOP Society questionnaire instruments
title: hitop — R package for HiTOP Society questionnaire instruments
text: Scores, validity-screens, distributes HiTOP Society instruments: PID-5 (FULL 220 / SF 100 / BF 25, one function per task version argument), HiTOP-SR (405), HiTOP-BR (45), HiTOP-HSUM (item data + export ; scoring pending Society feedback). Version 0.1.0, GPL-3. Maintained Jeffrey Girard.

title: hitop — R package for HiTOP Society questionnaire instruments
text: Document: Rscript -e 'devtools::document()' Test: Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' (testthat edition 3; currently fails locally Imports flextable/officer/snakecase installed) Check: Rscript -e 'devtools::check()' Coverage: Rscript -e 'covr::package_coverage()' Regenerate data: run matching script data-raw/; never edit data/*.rda R/sysdata.rda directly README: edit README.Rmd, Rscript -e 'devtools::build_readme()' Site: pkgdown, built/deployed .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml; local preview Rscript -e 'pkgdown::build_site()'

title: hitop — R package for HiTOP Society questionnaire instruments
text: Scoring signature: (data, items, [version,] srange, prefix, missing, calc_se, append = TRUE); always returns tibble. id/scales/tibble arguments; scales computed. Reliability separate returning family reliability_{pid5,hitopsr,hitopbr}(data, items, [version,] srange, alpha, omega) → per-scale tibble. Output columns: prefix + camelCase scale name (pid_anhedonia, hsr_...); validity scales prefix + abbreviation (pid_PNA, pid_INC); standard errors add _se. Scale membership comes *_scales list-column tables (pid_scales[[version]]$itemNumbers, hitopsr_scales, hitopbr_scales); reverse-keying validity-scale membership *_items tables. Never hardcode item numbers. User messaging via cli::cli_alert_warning() + cli::cli_alert_info() actionable {.code dplyr::filter(...)} suggestions; validation via validate_*()/cli_assert() R/util.R. Base-R data manipulation internally; {tibble} Import (default output), {lavaan} Suggests (needed calc_omega). Instrument administration text lives R/sysdata.rda (*_instructions); prebuilt DOCX/Qualtrics/REDCap artifacts inst/extdata/.

title: hitop — R package for HiTOP Society questionnaire instruments
text: Never hand-edit NAMESPACE, man/, data/*.rda, R/sysdata.rda — generated (roxygen2, data-raw scripts). Never change keying content (pid_items *_items/*_scales tables) without maintainer sign-; provenance cairn/SOURCES.md. New instruments follow pattern: items (+ scales) table via data-raw/ → score_* (+ validity_*) + generate_{docx,qualtrics,redcap}_* family (see cairn/DESIGN.md). Keep tests/, NEWS.md, _pkgdown.yml reference index step exported functions.

title: Scoring the HiTOP-BR
text: turn item-level ratings mean scores 8 scales, can use score_hitopbr() function. needs know object contains data columns contain item-level data. several ways can specify items. title: Scoring the HiTOP-BR
text: compute scale scores, can also estimate inter-item reliability using Cronbach’s α (alpha) McDonald’s ω (omega total). α fast widely used, assumes tau-equivalence (items load equally single factor); violations can make α - -estimate reliability. ω based congeneric single-factor model, allowing items different loadings error variances; typically provides accurate reliability estimate unit-weighted sums. assume scale essentially unidimensional; α ω coincide tau-equivalence holds. estimate reliability reliability_hitopbr() function, returns tibble one row per scale columns number items requested coefficients. default computes alpha omega; latter, need lavaan package installed (set omega = FALSE skip ).
code: reliability_hitopbr(   data = ku_hitopbr,   items = sprintf("hbr%02d", 1:45) ) #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>   scale            nItems alpha omega #>   <chr>             <int> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Antagonism            9 0.805 0.811 #> 2 Detachment            6 0.785 0.778 #> 3 Disinhibition         9 0.807 0.810 #> 4 Internalizing         7 0.825 0.827 #> 5 Somatoform            8 0.825 0.832 #> 6 Thought Disorder      6 0.731 0.739 #> 7 Externalizing        10 0.817 0.818 #> 8 P Factor             12 0.804 0.811

title: Scoring the HiTOP-SR
text: dataset collected item numbers standardized used arbitrary variable names, must rename columns standard format running scoring engine. rename_hitopsr_items() function handles preparation via two approaches.

title: Scoring the HiTOP-SR
text: columns labeled older pool names (e.g., HiTOP_659 Ext_432), use method = "original". function scans data frame columns, matches package’s built-item database, renames standard names.
code: # Setup a mock dataset with older legacy column names legacy_data <- data.frame(   HiTOP_659 = c(1, 2, 4),   HiTOP_301 = c(3, 4, 2),   ParticipantID = c(101, 102, 103) )  # Rename legacy columns using the native pipe standardized_legacy <-   legacy_data |>   rename_hitopsr_items(method = "original", prefix = "HSR_") #> Warning: Only 2 out of 405 HiTOP-SR items were successfully matched and renamed. #> ℹ Note: If you plan to use `score_hitopsr()`, ensure uncollected items exist in #>   the data frame as `NA` columns.  standardized_legacy #>   HSR_1 HSR_2 ParticipantID #> 1     1     3           101 #> 2     2     4           102 #> 3     4     2           103

what: Method 2: Matching via Literal Item Text
title: Scoring the HiTOP-SR
text: dataset uses completely customized column names tracked exact question prompts presented participants, use method = "text". Provide vector current column names alongside matching vector literal item text strings. Note: small mock examples contain subset full item pool, rename_hitopsr_items() safely issue cli warning letting know fewer 405 items matched. perfectly accommodates researchers intentionally administering short forms separate diagnostic modules.
code: # Setup a mock dataset with completely custom column names custom_data <- data.frame(   q_party = c(1, 2, 1),   q_flawless = c(4, 3, 4),   Age = c(21, 25, 30) )  # Define the mapping pairs my_cols <- c("q_party", "q_flawless") my_texts <- c(   "I preferred to stay home than to go to a party.",   "I felt that my work must be flawless." )  # Rename custom columns based on text matching standardized_custom <- custom_data |>   rename_hitopsr_items(     method = "text",     item_cols = my_cols,     item_text = my_texts,     prefix = "HSR_"   ) #> Warning: Only 2 out of 405 HiTOP-SR items were successfully matched and renamed. #> ℹ Note: If you plan to use `score_hitopsr()`, ensure uncollected items exist in #>   the data frame as `NA` columns.  standardized_custom #>   HSR_1 HSR_2 Age #> 1     1     4  21 #> 2     2     3  25 #> 3     1     4  30

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
text: Computes Cronbach's coefficient alpha set items unstandardized covariance matrix. Items coerced numeric calculation. Missing data can handled via pairwise deletion.

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
code: calc_alpha(df)

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
text: df data frame matrix one row per observation one column per item.

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
text: numeric scalar giving Cronbach's alpha (typically [0, 1], can outside range problematic data).

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
text: Let \(k\) number items let \(X_1,\ldots,X_k\) denote item scores. Define total score \(T = \sum_{=1}^k X_i\). Cronbach's alpha can written without matrix notation $$\alpha   \;=\; \frac{k}{k-1}\left(1 - \frac{\sum_{=1}^k   \mathrm{Var}(X_i)}{\mathrm{Var}(T)}\right).$$ words: take sum item variances divide variance total score; subtract ratio 1 multiply \(k/(k-1)\). \(\mathrm{Var}(X_i)\) \(\mathrm{Var}(T)\) computed using pairwise-complete observations. @section Coercion: column coerced via .numeric(). means: factors converted integer level codes (labels); characters parsable numbers become NA; dates/times become underlying numeric representations. Ensure columns truly numeric item responses pre-process needed.

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
text: Cronbach, L. J. (1951). Coefficient alpha internal structure tests. Psychometrika, 16(3), 297–334. Sijtsma, K. (2009). use, misuse, limited usefulness Cronbach's alpha. Psychometrika, 74(1), 107–120.

title: Compute Cronbach's Coefficient Alpha — calc_alpha
code: # Cronbach's alpha for the HiTOP-BR Detachment scale (items 7, 12, 30, 31, 36, 37) detach_items <- sprintf("hbr%02d", c(7, 12, 30, 31, 36, 37)) calc_alpha(ku_hitopbr[detach_items]) #> [1] 0.7847432

what: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
text: Computes McDonald's \(\omega_\mathrm{total}\) unidimensional scale using one-factor confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) fit lavaan using MLR estimation. Missing data can handled via full information maximum likelihood (FIML).

title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
code: calc_omega(df)

title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
text: df data frame matrix one row per observation one column per item.

title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
text: numeric scalar giving McDonald;s \(\omega_\mathrm{total}\) (typically [0, 1]; small estimation anomalies can yield values slightly outside range).

title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
text: model f =~ item1 + item2 + ... + itemK std.lv = TRUE (.e., \(\mathrm{Var}(f)=1\)). \(\omega = \frac{(\sum_i \lambda_i)^2} {(\sum_i \lambda_i)^2 + \sum_i \theta_i}\), \(\lambda_i\) unstandardized loadings \(\theta_i\) unstandardized residual variances indicators. function validates inputs fails informative messages CFA converge implied residual variances invalid. Mixed-sign loadings trigger warning (often indicates reverse-keying needed). Note latent variable estimated f, please avoid naming items .

title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
text: McDonald, R. P. (1999). Test Theory: Unified Treatment. Lawrence Erlbaum. Green, S. B., & Yang, Y. (2009). Reliability summed item scores using structural equation modeling: alternative coefficient alpha. Psychometrika, 74(1), 155–167. Zinbarg, R. E., Revelle, W., Yovel, ., & Li, W. (2005). Cronbach's α, Revelle's β, McDonald's ωH. Psychometrika, 70(1), 123–133.

title: Compute McDonald's Omega (total) via CFA — calc_omega
code: # McDonald's omega-total for the HiTOP-BR Detachment scale detach_items <- sprintf("hbr%02d", c(7, 12, 30, 31, 36, 37)) calc_omega(ku_hitopbr[detach_items]) #> [1] 0.7779365

title: Generate a REDCap Instrument ZIP File for the HiTOP-HSUM — generate_redcap_hitophsum
text: file Character string. destination path output ZIP file. Defaults "hitophsum_redcap.zip". form_name Character string. internal name form REDCap. Defaults "hitophsum_questionnaire". required Logical. Whether items marked required. Defaults TRUE. other_drug_rule Character string. symptom items gated "drug" substances (every substance except alcohol nicotine). default, "most_frequent", follows source module's looping rule: symptom items shown frequently used drug used least monthly (drugs whose frequency "Prefer say" ignored; two drugs tie highest frequency, symptom items shown ). "per_drug" loosens gate every drug used least monthly gets symptom items, regardless relative frequency. Alcohol nicotine symptom gating unaffected argument.

title: Score the Personality Inventory for DSM-5 Validity Scales — validity_pid5
text: full PID-5, score 17 higher INC indicative inconsistent responding. score 3 higher ORS indicative overreporting. score 10 lower PRD indicative positive impression management (underreporting rather genuine responding) whereas score 21 higher PRD indicative genuine responding (rather positive impression management). score 11 lower SD-TD indicative social desirability (rather defensiveness), whereas score 19 higher SD-TD indicative defensiveness (rather social desirability). PID-5-SF, scores 8 INC-S indicative inconsistent responding. Cut-scores ORS-S, PRD-S, SD-TD-S yet validated. PRD SD-TD cut scores raw sums compared fixed thresholds assume items coded 0-3. Unlike INC ORS scales, adapt srange, scoring items another scale (e.g. 1-4) mis-flags respondents; warning issued srange c(0, 3). BF version computes percent-missing (PNA) index cut-score scales.

title: hitop 0.2.0
text: release makes several breaking API changes stabilize interface CRAN submission. HiTOP-HSUM aligned authoritative source (HiTOP Society’s “revised SUD module-August 2024” development worksheet): hitophsum_items item text now matches worksheet’s substance-specific wording (alcohol items use drink-specific phrasing; nicotine -drug items corrected; obvious worksheet typos repaired logged), free-text nicotine quantity item now shows non-cigarette, non-cigar forms, hitophsum_choices gains alcohol/cigarette/cigar quantity choice sets. REDCap export, cigar quantity item now valid dropdown (previously imported empty choice list), “Prefer say” frequency responses longer satisfy symptom gate. New other_drug_rule argument generate_redcap_hitophsum(): default ("most_frequent") follows worksheet’s looping rule — symptom items appear frequently used drug used least monthly (ties show tied drugs) — "per_drug" reproduces previous looser behavior gating every drug independently. overview DOCX now says “Street opioids” (previously “Heroin/opiates”) “Goose bumps”, item matrix matches corrected wording; prebuilt DOCX REDCap files regenerated New reliability_pid5(), reliability_hitopsr(), reliability_hitopbr() functions return per-scale tibble (scale, nItems, alpha, omega). replace alpha omega arguments score_pid5(), score_hitopsr(), score_hitopbr(), printed reliability table side effect removed score_pid5(), score_hitopsr(), score_hitopbr() now take single missing argument place previous na.rm (, score_pid5(), apa_scoring) arguments. score_pid5(), missing = "apa" (default) applies APA missing-data/proration rule, "available" averages present items (old apa_scoring = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE), "complete" returns NA scale missing item (old na.rm = FALSE). score_hitopsr()/score_hitopbr() offer "available" (default) "complete". Default behavior unchanged rank_scales() gains name argument (default "top_scales") naming output column, previously hard-coded "". also gains reverse srange arguments: scales named reverse reflected via sum(srange) - value ranking, reverse-directioned scale (e.g. well-scale, higher = healthier) ranks “higher = elevated” metric scales tibble argument removed score_pid5(), score_hitopsr(), score_hitopbr(), validity_pid5(), rank_scales(); functions now always return tibble Documentation accuracy polish across scoring tutorials pkgdown instrument pages: corrected stale column/dataset names HiTOP-SR tutorial (leftovers earlier “HiTOP-PRO” draft), updated HiTOP-BR scale count (8, 7) PID-5 appended-column count (now includes 5 domains), finished previously “work progress” PID-5-BF tutorial, added missing Scale Reliability sections HiTOP-BR PID-5-BF tutorials, fixed mis-targeted REDCap “Import Instructions” link PID-5 download page, reconciled instrument download pages describes resources actually links Standardized item-text punctuation hitopsr_items (7 items) hitopbr_items (1 item): every item now ends period. affected items (HSR 5, 27, 30, 284, 314, 332, 382 HBR 41) lack period source instrument , 398 405 HiTOP-SR items one; omissions treated typographical oversights. derived *_scales/*_subscales tables prebuilt DOCX/Qualtrics/REDCap artifacts inst/extdata/ regenerated match
